name: voice-system-expert
description: Use when working with Quetrex's voice interface, OpenAI Realtime API, WebRTC, or echo cancellation. Knows Quetrex's specific voice architecture decisions and patterns. CRITICAL - prevents breaking working voice system.
capabilities: Guide OpenAI Realtime API integration for voice interfaces
Implement browser-based echo cancellation using WebRTC
Avoid common anti-patterns that break voice systems
Configure audio constraints for optimal voice quality
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only architectural guidance for voice system implementation. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or filesystem access. All 50 static findings are false positives caused by markdown documentation being misidentified as security-sensitive patterns.
valueStatement: Voice interface implementation requires careful architectural decisions to prevent audio feedback loops and ensure reliable echo cancellation. This skill provides expert guidance on proven voice architecture using OpenAI Realtime API and browser-based echo cancellation.
userTitle: Implement Voice Systems with Browser AEC
limitations: This skill provides guidance only, not executable code
Requires browser environment with WebRTC support
Does not include custom audio processing algorithms

useCases.title: Integrate Voice AI Features
useCases.description: Add conversational voice capabilities to AI applications using OpenAI Realtime API and WebRTC audio streaming

useCases.title: Build Audio Pipelines
useCases.description: Create reliable audio capture and playback systems using browser echo cancellation and proper media constraints

useCases.title: Design Voice Architecture
useCases.description: Establish voice system patterns that prevent echo feedback and work across all major browsers

promptTemplates.title: Basic Voice Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up a basic voice interface using OpenAI Realtime API with browser echo cancellation?

promptTemplates.title: Audio Constraints
promptTemplates.prompt: What audio constraints should I use for getUserMedia to enable proper echo cancellation?

promptTemplates.title: Fix Echo Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: My voice system has echo problems. How do I implement the correct browser AEC pattern?

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my voice system code and ensure it follows the always-on microphone + browser AEC pattern without common anti-patterns

bestPractices: Always use browser echo cancellation with echoCancellation: true in getUserMedia constraints
Keep microphone enabled throughout the entire conversation - never toggle track state
Route all audio through browser pipeline using HTMLAudioElement to maintain AEC
antiPatterns: Toggling microphone track on and off during conversation breaks AEC
Routing audio outside browser via AudioWorklet bypass breaks echo cancellation
Implementing custom echo cancellation reinvents the wheel and causes issues

faq.question: Which browsers support browser echo cancellation?
faq.answer: All major browsers support WebRTC echo cancellation including Chrome, Safari, Firefox, and Edge

faq.question: What is the maximum audio sample rate for voice?
faq.answer: Voice applications typically use 24000 Hz sample rate for optimal quality and performance

faq.question: How do I handle microphone permission errors?
faq.answer: Catch NotAllowedError for permission denials and NotFoundError when no microphone is available

faq.question: Does this skill store audio data?
faq.answer: No, this skill only provides guidance. Actual audio handling depends on your implementation

faq.question: Why is my voice system producing echo?
faq.answer: Echo usually occurs when audio leaves the browser pipeline or echoCancellation is disabled

faq.question: How is this different from other voice implementations?
faq.answer: This follows the industry-standard pattern used by ChatGPT, Zoom, and Google Meet with always-on mic
